techniques.evidence_quote: Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams
deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams, actions that resulted in the temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts

attack_chain.description: Former employee Ramesh leveraged retained AWS cloud credentials that were not revoked upon his resignation in April 2018, gaining unauthorized access to Cisco's AWS environment approximately five months after departure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: five months later, in September 2018, Ramesh accessed Cisco's cloud infrastructure hosted on Amazon's Web Services

attack_chain.description: Ramesh executed a script targeting Cisco's AWS infrastructure to programmatically identify and delete EC2 virtual machines hosting the Webex Teams service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Ramesh then proceeded to run a script that deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams

attack_chain.description: The script successfully deleted 456 EC2 virtual machines supporting Cisco Webex Teams, causing the temporary loss of over 16,000 customer accounts and a multi-week outage costing Cisco over $2.4 million.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: deleted 456 virtual machines that were supporting Cisco's video conferencing software WebEx Teams, actions that resulted in the temporary deletion of more than 16,000 Webex accounts

initial_access_vector: Former employee retained active AWS cloud account credentials after resignation in April 2018; no evidence of credential revocation or access deprovisioning
persistence_mechanism: No active persistence mechanism established — attacker leveraged a pre-existing, never-revoked cloud account from their period of employment
privilege_escalation: No privilege escalation required — retained credentials already carried sufficient permissions to delete EC2 instances at scale
